questions.question: Why are GPUs useful for training neural networks?
options: They have higher clock speeds than CPUs
They can run many matrix operations in parallel
They automatically choose better model architectures
They store source code more efficiently
questions.explanation: Neural network training is dominated by matrix operations. GPUs contain many parallel cores designed to run those operations at the same time.

questions.question: Which command is the standard first check for an NVIDIA GPU on a local machine?
options: gpu --status
nvidia-smi
torch.cuda.list_devices()
python --gpu
questions.explanation: nvidia-smi reports NVIDIA GPU status, memory use, driver information, temperature, and running processes.

questions.question: What does VRAM limit during model training?
options: Only the Python version you can run
The model size and batch size that fit on the GPU
The number of files git can track
The maximum internet bandwidth of a cloud instance
questions.explanation: VRAM is memory on the GPU. Model weights, activations, gradients, and batches must fit there during training.

questions.question: Why does a GPU benchmark call torch.cuda.synchronize() around timed work?
options: To install the CUDA driver
To make asynchronous GPU work finish before reading the timer
To move all tensors back to CPU memory
To reduce the matrix size automatically
questions.explanation: CUDA work is asynchronous from Python's point of view. synchronize() waits for queued GPU work to finish so timing is accurate.

questions.question: Using the fp16 rule of thumb, about how many parameters fit in 24 GB of VRAM before extra training overhead?
options: 6 billion
12 billion
24 million
48 billion
questions.explanation: fp16 uses about 2 bytes per parameter, so 24 GB holds roughly 12 billion parameters before activations, gradients, and optimizer state.

questions.question: When is Google Colab a good first choice?
options: When you need a quick GPU experiment and do not have a local NVIDIA GPU
When you need permanent storage for production training data
When you want to avoid using notebooks entirely
When your code must run without an internet connection
questions.explanation: Colab is useful for quick experiments and learning when local GPU hardware is unavailable. Serious long-running jobs may need a paid cloud GPU instead.
